text:
It might look kind of funny, this morning, to wear my overcoat on the platform, but I was so happy to display that pretty overcoat this church give me. I seen Brother Neville up here the other day, with that nice suit on, how it fit him so nicely, and I thought, well, I… It looked so nice, and the congregation talking about it, I thought, "I'll just wear my overcoat out on the platform."
You know, I believe we never grow up. We always… And I don't want to grow up. How about that, Brother Luther? Never want to grow up, we just want to always remain children.
[Brother Neville says, "Brother Branham, I thought you had on one of those attires like these higher-classed ministers. I just glanced the corner of my eye, and I thought maybe you'd put on a robe."]
Complimentary to that nice overcoat.
text_no:
Det kan se litt rart ut i dag morges å gå med overfrakken på plattformen, men jeg var så glad for å vise frem den fine overfrakken menigheten ga meg. Jeg så Bror Neville her oppe her om dagen, med den flotte dressen som passet ham så godt, og jeg tenkte, vel, det… Det så så fint ut, og menigheten snakket om det, så jeg tenkte, "Jeg vil bare ha på meg overfrakken på plattformen."
Du vet, jeg tror vi aldri vokser opp. Vi vil alltid… Og jeg vil ikke vokse opp. Hva sier du til det, Bror Luther? Aldri vil jeg vokse opp, vi vil bare alltid forbli barn.
[Bror Neville sier, "Bror Branham, jeg trodde du hadde på deg en av de antrekkene som disse høytstående forkynnerne bruker. Jeg så bare i øyekroken, og jeg trodde kanskje du hadde tatt på deg en kappe."]
En hyllest til den fine overfrakken.

text:
Well, I'll say I was needing one awful bad, and it's the best one I ever had. And I sure do appreciate it. And Brother Roy Roberson (I don't know whether he is here this morning or not), he had something to do with the selecting of it. And it was really a fine selection, and we are very, very pleased to have it.
And so we are very pleased to be back in the house of the living God, this morning, and to enjoy these wonderful times of fellowship around His precious Word.
text_no:
Jeg må si at jeg virkelig trengte en, og det er den beste jeg noen gang har hatt. Jeg setter stor pris på det. Og Bror Roy Roberson (jeg vet ikke om han er her i morges eller ikke) hadde noe å gjøre med utvelgelsen. Det var virkelig et godt valg, og vi er svært, svært fornøyd med å ha den.
Vi er også svært glade for å være tilbake i det levende Guds hus denne morgenen, og for å nyte disse vidunderlige stundene med fellesskap rundt Hans dyrebare Ord.

text:
And it's soon now, if the Lord willing, I've got to go overseas. And I guess you seen it in the Businessmen's Voice, that I leave for overseas this next month. And we will be soliciting all the prayers of the people, that they will pray for us while we are away. It's seemingly that overseas my meetings seems to be better, because it takes better there. In America…
I was telling Brother Mercier that I listened to… He gave me a record player, first, and it had the records on it, of some of my sermons. I knew then, if anybody ever listened to me, it would have to be God's grace to them, because I thought I could be a little better.
He is taping that, too. You can cut that part out, see. Or, he is holding his thumb down; l guess that's cutting it off.
text_no:
Snart, hvis Herren vil, må jeg reise utenlands. Jeg antar dere har sett det i Businessmen's Voice, at jeg reiser utenlands neste måned. Vi ber om alle folks bønner, slik at de vil be for oss mens vi er borte. Det synes som om møtene mine går bedre utenlands, kanskje fordi de tas mer seriøst der. I Amerika...
Jeg fortalte Bror Mercier at jeg hørte på… Han ga meg først en grammofonspiller, med noen av mine prekener på plater. Jeg visste da, hvis noen noen gang hørte på meg, måtte det være Guds nåde til dem, fordi jeg mente at jeg kunne ha vært litt bedre.
Han tar også opp dette. Du kan klippe den delen bort, forstår du. Eller, han holder tommelen nede; jeg antar det betyr at han stopper innspillingen.

text:
But I tell you, I was so surprised. The poorest preaching I ever heard in my life, was my own, that's right, on the record. It made me so nervous, I couldn't even eat my dinner. I got sick, got away from the table, couldn't sleep that night.
And went down in Kentucky, with Brother Wood yesterday. Coming back, I said, "Brother Wood, I don't see how that I ever get a person to come and hear me speak. It's so poorly, such repeating myself, and, oh, grammar missing, and punctuation---there isn't any." I just… I don't know. I said, "It encouraged me in one way, to know it has to be God, or nobody would come at all." That's right.
text_no:
Men jeg forteller dere, jeg ble så overrasket. Den dårligste forkynnelsen jeg noensinne har hørt, var min egen, på opptaket. Det gjorde meg så nervøs at jeg ikke klarte å spise middag. Jeg ble dårlig, måtte forlate bordet og kunne ikke sove den natten.
I går dro jeg til Kentucky sammen med Bror Wood. På vei tilbake sa jeg: "Bror Wood, jeg forstår ikke hvordan noen orker å komme og høre meg tale. Det er så dårlig, jeg gjentar meg selv, og grammatikk og tegnsetting mangler fullstendig." Jeg sa videre: "Det oppmuntret meg på en måte, for det må være Gud, ellers ville ingen komme." Det er riktig.

text:
So I was speaking to Brother Collins. I suppose he is in the building. And so I said to him… He come over, and was telling him about it. I said, "Brother Collins, honestly…" I said, "I'm pretty near thirty years old, as a preacher, and I certainly ought to know what a sermon is." I said, "That's the poorest I ever heard."
And he is a Methodist minister, and his brother is quite a man in the Methodist denomination. And he said, "Well," he said, "I will tell you, Brother Branham," said, "the punctuations and your sentences may not end right, and things like that, but," said, "did you ever think of that guy that preached on the day of Pentecost, that couldn't even sign his own name, his name was Peter?" Said, "I imagine that wasn't punctuated just right."
text_no:
Jeg snakket med Bror Collins. Jeg antar at han er i bygningen. Jeg sa til ham... Han kom bort og jeg fortalte ham om det. Jeg sa: "Bror Collins, ærlig talt..." Jeg sa: "Jeg har vært forkynner i nesten tretti år, og jeg burde absolutt vite hva en preken er." Jeg sa: "Det var den dårligste jeg noen gang har hørt."
Han er metodistprest, og hans bror er en viktig mann i metodistkonfesjonen. Han sa: "Vel," sa han, "jeg skal fortelle deg, Bror Branham," sa han, "tegnsettingene og setningene dine kan være feil, og lignende, men," sa han, "har du noen gang tenkt på den fyren som prekte på pinsedagen, som ikke engang kunne skrive sitt eget navn? Han het Peter." Han sa: "Jeg antar at det heller ikke var tegnsatt helt riktig."

text:
But, you see, what makes a man get his eyes on that: You listen to these radio broadcasts, and that's all wrote out, you see. And they can write it out and punctuate it, and things, because they're reading it.
I stood with Charles Fuller, preaching behind a trough about like that, for his platform. And everything that he said, he read it right down, number one, number two, number three, number four, like that, till he got all right out, and timed it. And it was all censored, radio censored, and everything.
And with Billy Graham, I seen his, and so forth, where they just stand there and speak that, just read it just as fast as they can read it, and it's all ready, so I guess you would get the punctuation.
text_no:
Men, ser du, hva får en mann til å fokusere på det: Du lytter til disse radioprogrammene, og alt er skrevet ned, forstå? De kan skrive det ut, punktuere det, og lignende, fordi de leser det opp.
Jeg stod med Charles Fuller, som forkynte bak en talerstol omtrent slik som denne, som hans plattform. Og alt han sa, leste han rett ned, nummer en, nummer to, nummer tre, nummer fire, slik, til han var ferdig, og det var tidsinnstilt. Alt var sensurert, radiogodkjent og alt mulig.
Og med Billy Graham, jeg så hans manuskript, og så videre, hvor de bare står der og taler, leser det like raskt som de kan, og alt er klart, så jeg antar at de får med seg punktueringen.

text:
But the trouble with me, I couldn't even read it. If I wrote it, I'm sure I couldn't read it. So it's amazing grace, isn't it, to see what He can do for us. But I am grateful to starting in now, ending up of this old year, to start a new year.
I listened to a prayer line, and I certainly wasn't satisfied with the prayer line, either, to listen to that. It's the first time I ever heard myself back like that, for a message, and I certainly was surprised. The prayer lines isn't run right. No, I think it'll come to pass that, after a while, it'll be so that everybody will have to know everything about them before they'll believe. And beginning the first of the year, I want to change that, and just start the prayer line wherever God speaks to me, "This person is out of line," or "something wrong"; I will stop with him. Let the rest of them go on through, because you don't get enough through like that, see. The people know where they are, and who they are, and what they've done. But they find something that's not lined up just right with God, that's the time to stop on that one, say, "This is the one," see. So, I think maybe there'll be some alteration done, Brother Leo, since I heard them, and I trust that God will help us in the coming year.
text_no:
Problemet med meg var at jeg ikke en gang kunne lese det. Hvis jeg skrev det, er jeg sikker på at jeg ikke kunne lese det. Så det er virkelig en nåde, ikke sant, å se hva Han kan gjøre for oss. Men jeg er takknemlig for å starte nå, ved slutten av dette gamle året, for å begynne et nytt år.
Jeg lyttet til en bønnekø, og jeg var absolutt ikke fornøyd med den. Det var første gang jeg hørte meg selv tilbake på den måten, for en melding, og jeg ble virkelig overrasket. Bønnekøen ble ikke håndtert riktig. Nei, jeg tror at etter hvert vil det bli slik at alle må vite alt om seg selv før de vil tro. Fra starten av det nye året vil jeg endre på dette. Jeg vil begynne bønnekøen når Gud taler til meg: "Denne personen er ute av linje," eller "det er noe galt"; da vil jeg stoppe med den personen. La resten gå gjennom, fordi man ikke får nok gjennom på den måten, skjønner du. Folk vet hvor de er, hvem de er, og hva de har gjort. Men når de finner noe som ikke stemmer helt med Gud, er det på tide å stoppe med den personen og si: "Dette er den," skjønner du.
Så, jeg tror kanskje det blir noen endringer, Bror Leo, etter å ha hørt dem. Jeg håper Gud vil hjelpe oss i det kommende året.

text: Now, I believe that tonight they got services here at the Tabernacle. And I am to be up here on 62, with Brother Ruddell, tonight. I understand that they're going to have a watch service here. And Brother Ruddell had asked me for that watch service, but thinking maybe I would come back at the Tabernacle for that night, because I've always tried to be at the Tabernacle on New Year's Eve, and I wanted to come back down here with the brethren here. So I kind of compromised a little, and I will be with Brother Ruddell tonight, out on 62, at the old 62 Club that's been converted into a church. And then Wednesday night will be back here at the watch service. And then Thursday we leave for Chicago, and then on, and on to Philadelphia, and then overseas.
text_no: I kveld skal det være møter her i Tabernaklet. Jeg skal være på 62 med Bror Ruddell i kveld. Jeg forstår at det blir en nattevaktsgudstjeneste her. Bror Ruddell hadde bedt meg delta på den, men jeg vurderte å komme tilbake til Tabernaklet den kvelden. Jeg har alltid forsøkt å være her på nyttårsaften, og jeg ønsket å tilbringe den med brødrene her. Derfor har jeg inngått et kompromiss: Jeg vil være med Bror Ruddell i kveld på 62, i den gamle 62 Klubben som er omgjort til en menighet. Så er jeg tilbake her onsdag kveld for nattevaktsgudstjenesten. Torsdag reiser vi til Chicago, videre til Philadelphia, og deretter til utlandet.

text:
And this morning, we don't want to keep you too long, because we got interviews, and the services go on.
And, say, since the last prayer service here, oh, I am very grateful for the results that came forth from the last service of prayer. It was certainly a wonderful thing that our Lord can do when His people get together. "They that will call upon the name of the Lord, assemble themselves together," I believe is the way it is, "and pray, then God will hear from heaven."
So let us bow our heads, just a moment now, as we look to Him to give to us the inspiration needed for this message.
text_no:
Denne morgenen ønsker vi ikke å holde dere for lenge, fordi vi har intervjuer og møtene fortsetter.
Siden forrige bønnekø her, må jeg si at jeg er svært takknemlig for resultatene som kom ut av den siste bønnetjenesten. Det er virkelig fantastisk hva Herren kan gjøre når Hans folk samles. "De som påkaller Herrens navn, samler seg sammen," tror jeg det står, "og ber, da vil Gud høre fra himmelen."
La oss bøye hodene våre et øyeblikk nå, mens vi ser til Ham for å få den inspirasjonen vi trenger for denne meldingen.

text:
Lord, Thou has been our refuge and strength in all generations. Our forefathers, before us, trusted in Thee, and were not confounded. They believed in Thy Holy name, and they were brought forth as shining lights; and we, looking upon them, as the poet has said, "Footprints upon the sands of time; our partings leave behind us, footprints on the sands of time." Then we see that those who trusted in Thee, always, without one time failing, come out right. Though they went through many deep waters and great trials and persecutions, but yet, in the end, Thou did always bring them out more than conquerors, because it is Thy promised Word that You would do this.
And we would pray, today, that You would bless this little church. Bless its pastor, our good brother, Brother Neville, and his family. We ask that You will just be with him in this coming year, and will anoint him greater and bless him in every way. Strengthen the health of his family, the little ones that are coming up. Keep Brother Neville strong and healthy.
text_no:
Herre, Du har vært vår tilflukt og styrke gjennom alle generasjoner. Våre forfedre stolte på Deg og ble ikke til skamme. De trodde på Ditt hellige navn og ble som lysende stjerner. I følge poeten, etterlot de seg "fotspor i tidens sand" som veivisere for oss. De som stolte på Deg, gikk alltid seirende ut, uansett hvor mange prøvelser og forfølgelser de gjennomgikk. Til slutt gjorde Du dem til mer enn overvinnere, som Ditt lovede Ord sier.
Vi ber i dag at Du vil velsigne denne lille menigheten. Velsign dens pastor, vår gode bror, Bror Neville, og hans familie. Vi ber at Du vil være med ham i det kommende året, salve ham rikere og velsigne ham på alle måter. Styrk helsen til hans familie og de små som vokser opp. Hold Bror Neville sterk og frisk.

text:
And bless this church and every member that's in it. The trustee board, how we love them as real gallant men of God; and the deacon board, they also are Thy gallant servants. And all the people that come here, we are grateful for them, Lord. Just does my heart good to know that this little old pond and weed patch, standing on the corner many years ago, has been made a lighthouse to the kingdom of God. And I pray, God, that it'll stand until Jesus comes. May many great souls that's been in here, Lord, come forth at that day, washed in the blood of the Lamb. Grant it.
And as we open this blessed Word of Thine this morning, Lord, by turning back the pages to read from it a text, and we know that You alone can give the context. And we pray, God, that You will anoint Your Word, and may it go right to the hearts of the people that would do them good. Make believers out of unbelievers; and strengthen Christians; and heal the sick; and give courage to the discouraged; and get glory unto Thyself. In order to do this, Lord, circumcise the lips that shall speak and the ears that shall hear. For we ask it in Jesus' name. Amen.
text_no:
Velsign denne menigheten og hvert medlem i den. Takk for forstanderskapet, vi elsker dem som ekte, modige menn av Gud. Og takk til diakonrådet, som også er Dine modige tjenere. Vi er takknemlige for alle som kommer hit, Herre. Det gleder hjertet mitt å vite at dette lille, gamle vannhullet og ugressflekken som stod på hjørnet for mange år siden, nå har blitt et fyrtårn for Guds rike. Jeg ber, Gud, at det skal stå til Jesus kommer igjen. Må mange store sjeler som har vært her, Herre, stå frem på den dagen, vasket i Lammets blod. Gi det.
Og når vi åpner Ditt velsignede Ord denne morgenen, Herre, ved å bla tilbake for å lese en tekst, vet vi at bare Du kan gi sammenhengen. Vi ber, Gud, at Du vil salve Ditt Ord, og at det må trenge rett inn i hjertene til folket og gjøre dem godt. Gjør troende ut av vantro; styrk kristne; helbred de syke; gi mot til de motløse; og gi Deg selv ære. For å oppnå dette, Herre, rens leppene som skal tale og ørene som skal høre. For vi ber i Jesu navn. Amen.

text:
Announcing a little text that fell on my heart since last Sunday. I was speaking upon the wise men coming to see Jesus, the Star they followed from the east to the west. While studying that, I hit a scripture, and then did not get to be here Christmas Eve because of some jail service, and so forth, I had to be at. I thought I would speak today upon the subject of: Why Little Bethlehem?
And I want to read out of the book of Micah, the prophet, one of the minor prophets, the 5th chapter and the 2nd verse. It reads like this:
But thou, Bethlehem Ephratah, though thou be little among the thousands of Judah, yet out of thee shall … come forth unto me that … the ruler … be the ruler in Israel; whose goings forth has been from … old, from everlasting.
I had a spot on the Scripture there, is the reason I couldn't make out what the word was, at the time.
text_no:
Jeg vil dele en liten tekst som kom til hjertet mitt forrige søndag. Jeg talte om de vise menn som kom for å se Jesus, og Stjernen de fulgte fra øst til vest. Mens jeg studerte dette, kom jeg over en skrift, men kunne ikke være her julaften på grunn av noen fengselstjenester som jeg måtte delta på. Derfor tenkte jeg å tale i dag om emnet: Hvorfor Lille Betlehem?
Jeg vil lese fra profeten Mikas bok, en av de mindre profetene, kapittel 5, vers 2. Den lyder slik:
Men du, Betlehem Efrata, som er liten til å være blant Judas tusener, av deg skal det utgå en ... som skal være hersker i Israel; Hans utgang er fra gammel tid, fra evighetens dager.
Jeg hadde et tomt område på skriften der, og det er grunnen til at jeg ikke kunne tyde ordet på det tidspunktet.

text:
Of all of the places that is in Palestine! And there is so many great cities, and its localities, cities that are seemingly much more known in the lore of history, and better fortified, bigger cities. Why should God choose little Bethlehem to be the birthplace of His Son? There is many that are greater. And for instance, the historical lore of Jerusalem, the proud Jerusalem, the capital of all of it, and it's one of the largest cities of Palestine. And then we wonder why God would pick on that little bitty town of Bethlehem, for the birthplace of His Son.
But as the Scriptures has said, "What God determines to do, it will be done." And God foreordained it to be that way or it never would have been that way. And there… The Scripture says, in the 15th chapter of Acts, that there is nothing by chance. God knowed everything. And it just didn't happen to happen that way. It just was that God made it that way.
text_no:
Av alle stedene i Palestina! Det finnes så mange store byer og lokaliteter som er mye bedre kjent i historien, bedre befestet og større. Hvorfor valgte Gud lille Betlehem som fødestedet for Sin Sønn? Det finnes mange større og mer betydningsfulle byer. For eksempel, det stolte Jerusalem med sin rike historie, hele regionens hovedstad og en av de største byene i Palestina. Så hvorfor valgte Gud nettopp den lille byen Betlehem som fødestedet for Sin Sønn?
Som Skriften sier: "Det Gud bestemmer, skal skje." Gud hadde forutbestemt det slik, ellers ville det aldri ha skjedd på den måten. Skriften sier i Apostlenes gjerninger, kapittel 15, at ingenting skjer ved en tilfeldighet. Gud visste alt, og det var ikke noe som bare tilfeldigvis skjedde slik. Det var Gud som bestemte det på denne måten.

text:
And then when we in our little finite minds begin to think over, "Why would the great King of heaven choose a little place like that, instead of the capital?" Instead of some…
Even there was many places who had greater spiritual background than what Bethlehem did. For instance, some of the places like Shiloh. Shiloh was an ancient worship place of Israel, where they all came year by year to this great place where the ark of the Lord rested. And wonder why, then, that He wouldn't be born at Shiloh?
text_no: Når vi med våre begrensede sinn begynner å tenke over: "Hvorfor valgte Himmelens store Konge et så lite sted som det, i stedet for hovedstaden?" Det var mange steder som hadde en større åndelig bakgrunn enn Betlehem. For eksempel var Shiloh et gammelt tilbedelsessted i Israel, hvor alle kom år etter år til dette store stedet hvor Herrens ark hvilte. Hvorfor ble Han da ikke født i Shiloh?

text:
Then there was Gilgal, another great spiritual place of worship. Why wouldn't God let Him be born then at Gilgal?
And there was another one, Zion. Zion was on the mountain top. And we wonder why that Jesus would not be born then in Zion, because it's been a great historical mark of where the Lord has blessed His people in the ages.
And it looks like that maybe He would have chosen Zion, or Gilgal, or Shiloh, or one of the other great places where there had been great blessings and great teachings.
And there was other great cities, such as Hebron. That was the place for a man who was wanting a city of refuge, a place of safety. There was Ramoth-gilead, also another place of refuge where the people could come, which would've been very appropriate for him to be born.
text_no:
Så hadde vi Gilgal, nok et stort åndelig sted for tilbedelse. Hvorfor lot ikke Gud Ham bli født der i Gilgal? 
Så var det Zion, som lå på fjelltoppen. Vi undrer oss over hvorfor Jesus ikke ble født der i Zion, ettersom det har vært et betydningsfullt historisk sted der Herren har velsignet Sitt folk gjennom tidene. 
Det virker som Han kanskje kunne ha valgt Zion, Gilgal, Shiloh, eller et av de andre store stedene hvor det har vært store velsignelser og lærdommer. 
Det var også andre store byer, som Hebron. Dette var stedet for en mann som søkte en tilfluktsby, et trygt sted. Der var også Ramot-Gilead, en annen tilfluktsby der folk kunne komme, som ville vært svært passende for Ham å bli født i.

text:
And maybe, if I would have been thinking it, I would have brought Him over to Kadesh-barnea, for there was the judgment seat, and a place of refuge. Perhaps I would have brought Him over to that country for His birthplace, or maybe we would have chosen some of the other cities.
But, you know, I'm so glad that even just little insignificant things in the Bible mean so much. I believe that it was Jesus that said this, that "You pass over … do the weightier matters of the law, rather, but pass over the little things." And sometimes it's the little things that holds the big things together. But, all in all, the great cogs is turning just exactly the way God ordained them to turn. There'll never be one miss its place. God has foreordained all things, and it must hit just exactly to that place.
text_no:
Hadde jeg tenkt på det, ville jeg kanskje ha brakt Ham til Kadesj-Barnea, for der var domsstolen og et tilfluktssted. Kanskje ville jeg ha brakt Ham til det landet som fødested, eller vi kunne valgt noen av de andre byene.
Men, du vet, jeg er så glad for at selv små, tilsynelatende ubetydelige detaljer i Bibelen betyr så mye. Jeg tror det var Jesus som sa: "Dere går forbi ... gjør de tyngre delene av loven, men overser de små tingene." Noen ganger er det de små tingene som holder de store tingene sammen. Alt i alt fungerer de store mekanismene akkurat slik Gud har forordnet dem. Ingenting vil noen gang bomme på sin plass. Gud har forutbestemt alle ting, og de må treffe akkurat der de skal.

text:
And when we get faith like that, and begin to think of who is behind all this. What is the main spring that's turning this great economy of God's? We find it's the Holy Spirit. Not left into the hands of men to do things; but in the hands of the Holy Spirit. And He is the main spring, that if He can get the rest of the instruments, it'll work just perfectly and keep exactly God's time.
And then we see that, and we wonder in our mind then, as we look at great things and how that we would have them; and then it gives us a lot of consolation today to think that, maybe if we are a little group of people, maybe if we are insignificant to the world and to the bigger churches of denominations, yet God uses those little simple things sometime.
text_no:
Når vi får tro på den måten og begynner å tenke på hvem som står bak alt dette, hva er hovedmekanismen som driver denne store økonomien til Gud? Vi finner ut at det er Den Hellige Ånd. Det er ikke overlatt til mennesker å utføre ting, men i hendene til Den Hellige Ånd. Og Han er hovedmekanismen som, hvis Han kan få resten av instrumentene til å fungere, vil alt virke perfekt og holde Guds tid nøyaktig.
Når vi ser dette, undrer vi oss i vårt sinn når vi ser på store ting og hvordan vi ønsker dem. Det gir oss mye trøst å tenke på at, selv om vi bare er en liten gruppe mennesker, ubetydelige for verden og for de større menighetene i konfesjonene, bruker Gud av og til de små og enkle tingene.

text:
For it is also written in the Scriptures, "Fear not, little flock, it's your Father's good will to give you the kingdom." What a consolation! I know that just as sure as Jesus had to be born in little Bethlehem, so will the little flock be the one that the Father will give the kingdom to, because it's written. And all Scripture is given by inspiration, and the Scriptures cannot be broken. They must be fulfilled. So it gives us that hope, to know that it'll be a little flock that will receive the kingdom, a little faithful flock of believers. I'm trusting to be one of those flock … or, in that little flock, I should say.
And then we know the story, most all of us are acquainted with the story of how Israel came into Palestine by promise of God. And we know that the great Joshua was the one who divided each tribe their portion.
text_no:
For det står også skrevet i Skriften: "Frykt ikke, lille flokk, for det er deres Fars gode vilje å gi dere riket." For en trøst! Jeg vet at like sikkert som Jesus måtte bli født i lille Betlehem, vil den lille flokken være den som Faderen gir riket, fordi det står skrevet. Og all Skrift er gitt ved inspirasjon, og Skriften kan ikke brytes. Den må oppfylles. Dette gir oss håp om at det er en liten flokk som vil motta riket, en liten trofast flokk av troende. Jeg stoler på å være en del av den flokken … eller, i den lille flokken, bør jeg si.
De fleste av oss kjenner historien om hvordan Israel kom inn i Palestina ved Guds løfte. Vi vet at store Josva var den som fordelte hver stamme deres del.

text:
And it's certainly… If we had the time of about from now until six o'clock tonight, to stay right on this subject, to line these things away, to get the real meaning out of them; which, we do not have---just about thirty, forty minutes. So we have to strike just the high points, trusting that the Holy Spirit, to you Bible readers, will place the rest of it into your heart, as we go along.
Did you know that those portions, how Joshua allotted them in Palestine, was given by inspiration? And those Hebrew mothers of those patriarchs, when the baby was coming forth and her in travailing pain, to deliver the child, she uttered the very spot that those patriarchs would settle down and be at the last days.
Talk about inspiration, this Bible is inspired! No matter how little, just every little scripture has such an outstanding thing in the great picture. All of it, every word is inspired, and on that hangs the destination of souls, for it's the Word of the immortal and eternal God.
text_no:
Og det er helt sikkert... Hadde vi hatt tiden fra nå til seks i kveld, kunne vi holdt oss til dette emnet, gjennomgått alt grundig og fått den fulle betydningen frem; men vi har bare rundt tretti til førti minutter. Så vi må fokusere på høydepunktene, i tillit til at Den Hellige Ånd vil plassere resten i hjertene deres, dere som leser Bibelen, mens vi går frem.
Visste dere at de delene, hvordan Josva fordelte landene i Palestina, ble gitt ved inspirasjon? Og de hebraiske mødrene til disse patriarkene, når barnet skulle fødes og de hadde fødselsveer, uttalte nøyaktig stedet hvor disse patriarkene skulle bosette seg i de siste dager.
Snakk om inspirasjon, denne Bibelen er inspirert! Uansett hvor liten, hver eneste skrift har en fremtredende rolle i det store bildet. Alt, hvert eneste ord er inspirert, og på det henger sjelenes bestemmelse, for det er Ordet til den udødelige og evige Gud.

text:
And even those mothers, when the baby was born and she uttered their voice, placed them positionally where they would be in the promised land, hundreds of years later. And Joshua, not knowing that, yet by the same inspiration placed them exactly where they was to be.
And Joshua, dividing up, divided Judah's portion. If you'll notice on the map, it's geographically just west of the plain sea, a few miles south of Jerusalem, the capital. And when Judah was getting her part … or, his part, rather, in the land (his province, we would call it), it's strange, but this little city was not even mentioned: Bethlehem. Yet it was there, because that Abraham… Or, I believe it was Rebekah was buried at that place. But it must have been just a little village of some sort, because if you read Joshua 5, you'll find that there was a hundred and fifteen major cities under the domain of Judah, besides the villages and little towns; a hundred and fifteen cities, mentioned. And perhaps when it was divided up, that Bethlehem was so little, maybe just a little house or two, it wasn't even mentioned in the inheritance. And then we find out that it never actually come to be known…
text_no:
Selv de mødrene, når barnet ble født og hun ytret sin stemme, plasserte dem posisjonelt der de skulle være i det lovede land, hundrevis av år senere. Og Josva, uten å vite det, men likevel ved samme inspirasjon, plasserte dem nøyaktig der de skulle være.
Da Josva fordelte landområdene, delte han Judas del. Hvis du ser på kartet, ligger det geografisk rett vest for havslettene, noen få mil sør for Jerusalem, hovedstaden. Og da Juda fikk sin del av landet (sin provins, som vi ville kalle det), er det merkelig at denne lille byen ikke engang ble nevnt: Betlehem. Likevel eksisterte den, fordi Abraham … eller, jeg tror det var Rebekka som ble begravet der. Men det må ha vært bare en liten landsby av noe slag. Hvis du leser Josva 5, vil du se at det var 115 større byer under Judas domene, i tillegg til landsbyene og småstedene; 115 byer som er nevnt. Kanskje da landet ble delt, var Betlehem så liten, kanskje bare et hus eller to, at den ikke engang ble nevnt i arven. Og mange år senere ble det kjent...

text: The one that founded it was Caleb's son, whose name was Salma, and he founded it. The Bible said he was the father of it, which means he was the founder of Bethlehem. In other words, he must have moved in there and started some sort of businesses, and commercial, and the trading, and so forth, that grew it up. And later we will find out that the real reason of it, that the whole lands was jealous of that little piece of land; which lays to the north, and east, and slopes a little to the south, on that spur. And it was the most fertile of all of Palestine. It was a corn belt and a wheat belt, and there were great olive orchards, and so forth, on it, in that part of Bethlehem … or, Palestine, the end of Judah province.
text_no: Grunnleggeren var Calebs sønn, Salma. Bibelen sier at han var far til Bethlehem, som betyr at han grunnla byen. Med andre ord må han ha flyttet dit og startet ulike virksomheter, handel og lignende som bidro til byens vekst. Senere vil vi se at den egentlige årsaken til at hele området var sjalu på dette lille jordstykket, var at det lå mot nord og øst, og skrånet litt mot sør, på en utløper. Dette området var det mest fruktbare i hele Palestina. Det var en korn- og hveteåker med store oliventrær, beliggende i den delen av Bethlehem, eller rettere sagt Palestina, i enden av Judea-provinsen.

text: And we find out that it was also became the home of the harlot Rahab. When Israel had passed over the borderline of the Jordan River, into Palestine, we are acquainted with the story of Rahab the harlot. Let's picture her this morning for a few minutes now, as a young lady, a beautiful young woman whose some misfortune in life had been forced in, being a pagan, had been forced into the life that she was living. And many times people are forced into the life that they live.
text_no: Vi ser at det også ble boligen til skjøgen Rahab. Da Israel krysset grensen av Jordan-elven og kom inn i Palestina, blir vi kjent med historien om Rahab, skjøgen. La oss forestille oss henne denne morgenen, som en ung, vakker kvinne som på grunn av uheldige omstendigheter og som hedning ble tvunget inn i livet hun levde. Ofte blir mennesker tvunget inn i det livet de lever.

text: I was in the prisons the other night, to see a man who was taking a sentence. And I took him by the hand, and talked to him. And I said, "Why would you do such a thing as that?" And holding me by the hand, he begin to speak. And he was forced into what he was doing. I said, "Because that you permitted that to force you to that. You don't have to do that. No man has to drink." I said, "I am nervous, myself, but there is no need of that."
text_no: Jeg besøkte fengselet forleden kveld for å møte en mann som sonet en dom. Jeg tok ham i hånden og snakket med ham. Jeg spurte: "Hvorfor ville du gjøre noe slikt?" Han holdt meg i hånden og begynte å forklare at han var blitt tvunget til det han gjorde. Jeg sa: "Du lot det presse deg til å gjøre det. Du trenger ikke gjøre det. Ingen mann må drikke." Jeg fortsatte: "Jeg er også nervøs, men det er ikke nødvendig."

text: And this young woman, after she had got her first hearing of Israel, and of a God who was a God that answered prayer; not only a God to pray to, but a God that answered back. When she heard that there was a God of miracles, who could perform miracles, who dried up the seas and rained bread out of the heavens, her heart begin to tremble. And when she got the first message from two preachers that went over, quickly she accepted it with all her heart. And there was a scarlet cord bound at her window, for a protection of her house, because that she had received the message.
text_no: Denne unge kvinnen, etter at hun først hørte om Israel og om en Gud som svarte på bønn; ikke bare en Gud å be til, men en Gud som svarte tilbake. Da hun hørte at det fantes en Gud med mirakler, som kunne utføre mirakler, som tørket opp havene og lot brød regne fra himmelen, begynte hjertet hennes å skjelve. Og da hun fikk den første meldingen fra to forkynnere som kom over, aksepterte hun den raskt og helhjertet. Det ble bundet en skarlagenssnor ved vinduet hennes som beskyttelse for huset hennes, fordi hun hadde mottatt budskapet.

text:
I might add here: Did you know she typed the Gentile church? She was a Gentile, and she was a type of the Gentile church, when they heard the message. We were all out in spiritual prostitution, committing spiritual fornications against the God of heaven, in all kinds of stuff, all kinds of denominations and religions. But when we heard that there was a God that still lived, that could perform miracles, quickly we received the message.
There was applied the blood of the Lord Jesus, which made the scarlet cord. And to keep from going into detail, you know how she hung it from her window, publicly; the blood was displayed publicly. That's the way the blood has got to be displayed, publicly hanging from the outside of the wall, to show that, on the inside, something had taken place. That's the way the true believer in Christ is this morning; on the outside is the display of the blood of the Lord Jesus, that shows something took place on the inside.
text_no:
Visste du at hun representerte den hedenske menigheten? Hun var en hedning og en symbolsk representasjon av den hedenske menighet, da de hørte budskapet. Vi var alle i åndelig prostitusjon, begikk åndelig utroskap mot himmelens Gud, i alle slags ting, alle slags konfesjoner og religioner. Men da vi hørte at det fantes en Gud som fortsatt levde, som kunne utføre mirakler, tok vi raskt imot budskapet.
Herrens Jesu blod ble anvendt, noe som dannet den skarlagensfargede snoren. For å unngå detaljer, vet du hvordan hun hang den fra sitt vindu, offentlig; blodet ble offentlig vist. Slik må også blodet vises offentlig, hengende fra utsiden av veggen, for å vise at noe hadde skjedd på innsiden. Slik er den sanne troende i Kristus denne morgenen; på utsiden vises Herrens Jesu blod, som viser at noe har skjedd på innsiden.

text: And through this, God looked down when the wrath fell and the trumpets begin to blow, God seen that scarlet cord hanging there as a memorial. It's always pleased Him to pass over the blood. "When I see the blood, I will pass over you." He saw it. And when the shaking and the Holy Spirit rumbled the earth and shook down them walls---some twenty-feet thick---not one rock fell where that cord was hanging. Shows the protection of a true God to a true believer, no matter what state you're in when He finds you, if you'll just accept that scarlet cord. It weaves through the Bible.
text_no: Gud så ned da vreden falt og trompetene begynte å blåse. Han så det skarlagensrøde tauet som hang der som et minnesmerke. Det har alltid gledet Ham å gå forbi der hvor blodet er. "Når Jeg ser blodet, vil Jeg gå forbi deg." Han så det. Og da Den Hellige Ånd rystet jorden og rev ned veggene - som var rundt seks meter tykke - falt ikke en eneste stein der hvor tauet hang. Dette viser beskyttelsen fra en sann Gud til en sann troende, uansett hvilken tilstand du er i når Han finner deg, hvis du bare aksepterer det skarlagensrøde tauet. Dette temaet går som en rød tråd gjennom hele Bibelen.

text: And then we see her when she was taken in as one of the Israelites. She fell in love with a man, which was a captain and a prince in Judah. He was a captain of the Israelite armies. His name was Salmon, just like the king, Solomon. And he was a captain, and she become in a great romance with this captain, which was the prince of Judah. And finally she married him. And when the estate was settled up for the Israelites, she and her beloved husband lived in Bethlehem.
text_no: Vi ser henne også da hun ble tatt inn blant israelittene. Hun forelsket seg i en mann, en kaptein og prins i Juda. Han var kaptein for de israelske hærene og het Salmon, lik kongen, Salomo. Hun utviklet en stor romanse med denne kapteinen, som var prins av Juda. Til slutt giftet hun seg med ham. Da godset ble oppgjort for israelittene, bosatte hun og hennes elskede ektemann seg i Betlehem.

text:
Now you begin to see it open, haven't you? See, it begin to open as we see that in Bethlehem she lived, being a Gentile bride, to a Jew. Why? Because she believed in a miracle-working God. And as… Look what she come from, from being out of a house of ill fame, of prostitution; through her conversion and through her unfailing faith in God, it brought her from a prostitute house, to a beautiful home in Bethlehem. What a difference!
That's the way it does all of us. From a house of unbelief and flusterations, and immoral acts, and everything; to a place, position in Christ, which is most beautiful. From the ridiculous to the sublime, that's the difference that it makes through our conversion. And did you see, she married a prince of the house of Judah, a captain? That captain represented Christ, took to Himself a Gentile bride. From the lowest of lowest, to the principal and best place in the land, as we will get to it later in our message, to prove that it was nothing else could be but that. They're a type of the Gentile church.
text_no:
Ser du at det begynner å åpne seg, ikke sant? Hun bodde i Betlehem og var en hedensk brud gift med en jøde. Hvorfor? Fordi hun trodde på en Gud som gjør mirakler. Se hva hun kom fra: et dårlig rykte, fra prostitusjon; gjennom hennes omvendelse og urokkelige tro på Gud, ble hun reddet fra et hus med dårlig rykte til et vakkert hjem i Betlehem. For en forskjell!
Slik fungerer det for oss alle. Fra et hus av vantro, forvirring og umoralske handlinger, til en posisjon i Kristus, som er utrolig vakker. Fra det latterlige til det opphøyde, det er den forskjellen omvendelse gjør. Hun giftet seg med en prins fra Juda hus, en kaptein. Denne kapteinen representerte Kristus, som tok seg en hedensk brud. Fra det laveste av det lave til den viktigste og beste plass i landet, som vi skal se på senere i vår melding, for å bevise at det ikke kunne være annerledes. De er en type på den hedenske menighet.

text:
And we find out they had a lovely home in Bethlehem, as Salmon had established it, and it would become a great place, and the fertile lands. And how beautiful it is to think of that great wheat country there, that it would be the bread place of the world. And, it is true! No wonder Jesus had to be born there, because He was the bread of life! There is where all the nation come for their wheat, all the nations come for their corn. For, it was in Bethlehem, that had those fertile lands. And, you see, this little bitty thing, just saying, "Oh, it was the province of wheat," yet that meant something. And you see how the Gentile prince … or, this Jewish prince taking His Gentile bride back up to Bethlehem, for a settling down place, a place to live, where there was plenty of bread.
We find out, through this great romance and great obedience to faith, through the harlot Rahab, she gave birth to a son to Salmon, and his name was Boaz. And we all are acquainted with another great story tying in this link here now. And Boaz was born in Bethlehem, from Salmon and the harlot Rahab.
text_no:
Vi oppdager at de hadde et vakkert hjem i Betlehem, etablert av Salmon, og det ville bli et flott sted med fruktbare marker. Hvor vakkert er det ikke å tenke på dette store hvetelandet der, som skulle bli verdens brødkammer. Og det er sant! Ikke rart at Jesus måtte bli født der, for Han var livets brød! Det var der alle nasjonene kom for sin hvete og sitt korn, for Betlehem hadde disse fruktbare områdene. Dette lille punktet, "Oh, det var hveteprovinsen," betyr noe. Se hvordan den jødiske prinsen tar Sin hedenske brud tilbake til Betlehem, for å slå seg ned og bosette seg, der det var rikelig med brød.
Gjennom denne store kjærlighetshistorien og troens lydighet, ser vi at skjøgen Rahab fødte en sønn for Salmon, og hans navn var Boas. Vi er alle kjent med den andre store historien som knytter seg til denne lenken nå. Boas ble født i Betlehem av Salmon og skjøgen Rahab.

text:
And we find out that years later, after Naomi had left the country and gone over to sojourn with the Moabites, and when they had evilly mistreated her and she had got out of fellowship with the rest of the believers, and associated herself in a backslidden condition, in another land, among another people. In otherwise, she had left the true believing church, to go out into the world for a little while, to join some social church, who believed in just anything would be all right. In there, she lost her husband.
And it don't have to be every time in physical death. You might lose him in a spiritual death, or lose your wife. Better stay on good grounds! You had better stay where you know you are covered by the blood, regardless of what the rest of them looks like, how big they are, how fine of spires they have on their churches, or how the big bells ring. You better stay where the blood covers the sins of the people. You might lose one another, and, above all, you might lose the Lord Jesus and be barred out.
And we find out then that after her sons was gone (she lost both of them), and she returned back because there was no revival spirit in the city, at the time.
text_no:
Vi ser at flere år senere, etter at Naomi hadde forlatt landet og reist for å oppholde seg hos moabittene, ble hun ondsinnet behandlet og mistet fellesskap med de andre troende. Hun befant seg i en frafallen tilstand i et annet land, blant et annet folk. Med andre ord, hun hadde forlatt den sanne menigheten for en stund for å slutte seg til en sosial menighet som trodde at hva som helst var greit. Der mistet hun sin mann. Dette trenger ikke alltid å bety fysisk død. Du kan miste ham i en åndelig død, eller miste din hustru. Det er bedre å holde seg på trygg grunn! Du gjør klokt i å bli der du vet at du er dekket av blodet, uansett hvordan de andre ser ut, hvor store deres menigheter er, hvor fine spirene på kirkene deres er, eller hvordan de store klokkene ringer. Du gjør best i å bli der hvor blodet dekker folkets synder. Du kan miste hverandre, og, viktigst av alt, du kan miste Herren Jesus og bli utestengt.
Vi ser så at etter at hennes sønner hadde gått bort (hun mistet begge), vendte hun tilbake fordi det ikke var noen vekkelsesånd i byen på den tiden.

text: Oh, how I would love to rest here for a few minutes! How I'd like to pick it up right here and show you! Regardless of how many of the church has gone back, stay under the blood; that's the place to abide. In or out, up or down, thick or thin, wherever it may be, stay under the blood. But Naomi, she thought it would be better to go over and join another group, because they were having troubles, spiritual bread wasn't there. But God will restore it back, "I will restore, saith the Lord." Stay in the field where it comes from.
text_no: Å, hvor jeg skulle ønske jeg kunne hvile her noen minutter! Hvor gjerne jeg skulle ønske å kunne vise deg dette akkurat her! Uansett hvor mange i menigheten som har vendt tilbake, bli under blodet; det er stedet å bli. I eller ute, opp eller ned, tykt eller tynt, uansett hvor det måtte være, bli under blodet. Men Noomi tenkte at det ville være bedre å gå over og slutte seg til en annen gruppe, for de hadde problemer, og det fantes ikke åndelig brød der. Men Gud vil gjenopprette det, "Jeg vil gjenopprette, sier Herren." Bli på marken hvor det kommer fra.

text:
So, we find that she begin to long to go back, because they heard that a great revival had broke out. And if you noticed, Naomi returned in "barley season," the Bible said, just at the time of the harvest; in other words, when there was a great revival going on---spiritually applying it. She returned just in that season. Nothing left; she had nothing.
And Orpah, one of her sons' wives, when she come to look upon what she had to sacrifice, being a type of the modern church, "If I have to go over there, I'll have to get away from my dances, I'll have to get away from my big time, and my social gatherings." Then she just wept and kissed her mother-in-law, and returned back.
text_no:
Så vi ser at hun begynte å lengte etter å dra tilbake fordi de hadde hørt at en stor vekkelse hadde brutt ut. Og hvis du la merke til det, returnerte Naomi i "byggsesongen," som Bibelen sier, akkurat i høsttiden; med andre ord, når det var en stor åndelig vekkelse. Hun vendte tilbake akkurat i denne sesongen. Hun hadde ingenting igjen; hun hadde ingenting.
Og Orpah, en av hennes sønners koner, da hun så hva hun måtte ofre, var en type på den moderne menigheten. "Hvis jeg må gå dit, må jeg gi opp dansene mine, jeg må gi opp de store tidene mine og mine sosiale sammenkomster." Da gråt hun bare, kysset sin svigermor, og vendte tilbake.

text:
But here is a beautiful picture: There was one named Ruth, with her, another daughter-in-law, which was a type again of the Gentile bride, who kissed her mother-in-law, and said, "I will forsake everything. I'm going with you. Let your people be my people. Let your God be my God. Wherever you die, there I will die. Where you are buried, there I will be buried." That's it. That's what God wants. It's not that borderline, half way; but an absolute, full surrender to the kingdom of God. She kissed her.
And so Naomi said, to discourage her, said, "You had better go back to your people. I'm old, and there is no more sons in me." And the law was that she had to wait for a son. And said, "Then there is no more in me. And if I would have a husband, and I'd have a son, you would be too old to marry him, so you just return back to your people."
But Ruth said, "I will not go back!" Faith---rooted, grounded, perfect faith---had come into Ruth's heart. She said, "I'm going right with you." And she clave to her, held onto her. "I'm going to be where you are." I like that.
text_no:
Her er et vakkert bilde: Der var en ved navn Rut, sammen med sin svigermor, som igjen var en type på den hedningebruden, som kysset sin svigermor og sa: "Jeg vil forlate alt. Jeg går med deg. Ditt folk skal være mitt folk. Din Gud skal være min Gud. Hvor du dør, der vil jeg dø. Hvor du blir begravet, der vil jeg også bli begravet." Akkurat slik ønsker Gud det. Ikke halvveis eller på grensen, men en absolutt, full overgivelse til Guds rike. Hun kysset henne.
Da sa Naomi, for å motvirke henne: "Du bør heller dra tilbake til ditt folk. Jeg er gammel, og det er ingen flere sønner i meg." Loven var at hun måtte vente på en sønn. "Og hvis jeg skulle få en ektemann og få en sønn, vil du være for gammel til å gifte deg med ham, så du bør bare dra tilbake til ditt folk."
Men Rut svarte: "Jeg vil ikke dra tilbake!" Tro—rotfestet, grunnfestet, perfekt tro—hadde kommet inn i Ruts hjerte. Hun sa: "Jeg går med deg." Og hun klynget seg til henne, holdt fast ved henne. "Jeg skal være hvor du er." Det liker jeg.

text:
And we're acquainted with the story as it goes on, that this great Boaz, which was the lord of the harvest, was in harvest at the time, and was a kinsman to Naomi. And when she found Ruth out there in a little mission, gleaning, every little straw that she could pick up, that had some wheat on it, she held to it, for it was life. Boaz, being the lord of the harvest, he commanded that his reapers would drop a handful now and then, for her; and she would pick it up, and with joy. And she shucked out a great apronful of it, that day. And when Boaz, the lord of the harvest, came out and looked upon Ruth, and seen her faithfulness, he fell in love with her.
Watch Boaz represent Christ. Where was he at? Bethlehem. Where was Ruth come to? Bethlehem. Where was she gleaning at? In Bethlehem. See all those spiritual significances to this here, the background to this great scene that's taking place, God knowing it in the beginning?
text_no:
Vi kjenner historien videre, der den store Boas, som var herre over høsten, var midt i innhøstingen og en slektning av Naomi. Da hun fant Ruth på en liten misjon, der Ruth sanket hvert strå med litt hvete, klamret hun seg til det, for det var livsviktig. Boas, som herre over høsten, befalte sine høstfolk å slippe en håndfull hvete nå og da, for hennes skyld, og hun samlet det opp med glede. Den dagen fylte hun et helt forkle med hvete. Da Boas, herre over høsten, kom ut og så Ruths trofasthet, ble han forelsket i henne.
Se hvordan Boas representerer Kristus. Hvor var han? I Betlehem. Hvor kom Ruth? Til Betlehem. Hvor sanket hun? I Betlehem. Legg merke til alle de åndelige betydningene i bakgrunnen til denne store scenen som utspiller seg, Gud kjente til dette fra begynnelsen.

text:
And she begin to question to her mother-in-law, what she must do. And finally Ruth was married to Boaz, a Gentile married to a prince again, in Judah, and settled down and lived in Bethlehem. "Oh, thou little Bethlehem, … aren't you the least among all the principal cities of Palestine; but it's pleased God whose knowing and going forth was from old, from the beginning, to have His Son born there."
He knows all things, and He works it just to be right. And there Ruth married Boaz. And when Ruth and Boaz was married; if we had time to go into the great story there, which was one of the greatest love scenes of all ages, when Ruth and Boaz were married. And you remember…
Let's just stop for a minute here. Just too good to pass over!
text_no:
Hun begynte å rådføre seg med sin svigermor om hva hun skulle gjøre. Til slutt giftet Ruth seg med Boaz, en ikke-jøde som ble prins i Juda, og bosatte seg i Betlehem. "Å, lille Betlehem, er du ikke den minste av alle hovedbyene i Palestina, men det gledet Gud at Hans Sønn skulle bli født der."
Han vet alle ting og tilrettelegger alt på den riktige måten. Ruth giftet seg med Boaz. Hvis vi hadde tid, kunne vi dykke ned i den fantastiske historien der, som er en av de største kjærlighetshistoriene gjennom tidene, når Ruth og Boaz ble gift. Og husk...
La oss stoppe et øyeblikk. Dette kan vi ikke la gå upåaktet hen!

text:
Ruth was a Gentile. She had no inheritance with the Jew, just like we Gentiles had no inheritance. So, Naomi was actually the one to inherit. So, she had lost all of her first estate; all of her goods had been sold at public auction. Therefore, she was … been excommunicated, and had gone away.
Now when she came back, there was only one person who could ever redeem her lost inheritance; that was a kinsman right next to her. And Boaz knew this, so he had to work some way to get this Gentile girl for a wife. And what did he have to do? He had to buy all of the estate of Naomi, in order to get in this estate, which Ruth was part of the estate of Naomi. And the only man that could buy it, would have to be a person that was kinfolks, near kinsman. That was the law of redemption.
text_no:
Ruth var en hedning og hadde ingen arv hos jødene, akkurat som vi hedninger heller ikke hadde noen arv. Naomi var egentlig den som skulle arve. Hun hadde imidlertid mistet hele sin opprinnelige eiendom; alle hennes eiendeler var blitt solgt på offentlig auksjon. Derfor var hun blitt ekskommunisert og hadde reist bort. 
Da hun kom tilbake, var det kun én person som kunne løse inn hennes tapte arv, nemlig en nær slektning. Boas visste dette, og han måtte finne en måte å få denne hedenske jenta som kone. Hva måtte han gjøre? Han måtte kjøpe hele eiendommen til Naomi for å få tilgang til denne eiendommen, der Ruth var en del av Naomis eiendom. Og den eneste personen som kunne kjøpe den, måtte være en nær slektning. Det var innløsningsloven.

text: And the only way that Christ could ever buy the estate of the backslidden Israel, was to become a Kinsman. Only way God could redeem the human race, God Himself had to be made flesh. And Jesus was God made kinfolks to the human race. He was Emmanuel. He become kinfolks. He took upon Him, not the form of angels, but the form of a servant who washed the feet, and lived… And the foxes had holes, and the birds of the air had nests, but He didn't even have a place to lay His head. He eat, He drink, He cried, He laughed, just like other men. And He was God, not a prophet. He was God because He had to be kinfolks in order to redeem the lost human race. So, Boaz, in this great type at Bethlehem; look where this Kinsman was born to the human race---had to be.
text_no: Den eneste måten Kristus kunne kjøpe tilbake gården til det frafalne Israel på, var ved å bli en slektning. Den eneste måten Gud kunne forløse menneskeslekten på, var at Gud Selv måtte bli kjød. Jesus var Gud gjort til slektning av menneskeheten. Han var Emmanuel. Han ble vår slektning. Han tok ikke på seg englers form, men formen av en tjener som vasket føtter og levde blant oss. Reven hadde huler, og fuglene under himmelen hadde reir, men Han hadde ikke en gang et sted å legge sitt hode. Han spiste, drakk, gråt og lo som andre mennesker. Og Han var Gud, ikke en profet. Han var Gud fordi Han måtte være slektning for å forløse den tapte menneskeslekten. Boaz, i denne flotte typologien fra Betlehem, viser hvor denne Slektningen ble født for menneskeheten - det måtte være slik.

text: And then when this great Boaz redeemed her, he had to make a public showing that he had redeemed all of her lost estate. So he went to the gate of Bethlehem, that little city again, and he called the elders of the city, and he let them know that that day he had bought everything that Naomi had lost. Everything that she lost, he bought it back. And he kicked off his shoe and threw it up before the people, as an ensign, "And if there is anyone has any reason to say something, say it now, for this is a memorial that I have redeemed everything that she lost."
text_no: Da denne store Boas løste henne ut, måtte han vise offentlig at han hadde gjenvunnet hele hennes tapte eiendom. Han gikk derfor til byporten i Betlehem og kalte til seg byens eldste, for å informere dem om at han den dagen hadde kjøpt tilbake alt som Naomi hadde mistet. Alt hun hadde tapt, kjøpte han tilbake. Han tok av seg skoen og kastet den foran folk som et tegn: "Om noen har grunn til å protestere, si det nå, for dette er et minne om at jeg har løst inn alt det hun har mistet."

text: Oh, blessed be the name of the Lord! And when our Kinsman came, Jesus of Nazareth, born in Bethlehem, He stood on top of Golgotha, and lifted Him up between the heavens and earth, as a memorial that He had redeemed everything that the human race had lost in the fall. How can men despise divine healing and the powers of the resurrection of the Lord Jesus, when a public ensign was made at Calvary, that "I have redeemed the whole human race and everything that they ever lost," redeemed our soul, redeemed our body, redeemed everything that we lost in the fall. Our Kinsman Redeemer came and was made flesh, and dwelt among us, and offered up the ensign, and said, "It's finished." What's finished? Everything is finished. We're just walking right into our inheritance. And as the days go on, we're walking closer and closer.
text_no: Å, velsignet være Herrens navn! Da vår slektning, Jesus fra Nasaret, født i Betlehem, kom, stod Han på Golgata og ble løftet opp mellom himmel og jord som et minnesmerke om at Han hadde gjenløst alt som menneskeheten mistet ved fallet. Hvordan kan mennesker forakte guddommelig helbredelse og oppstandelseskraften fra Herren Jesus, når det ble gjort et offentlig tegn på Golgata, som sa: "Jeg har gjenløst hele menneskeslekten og alt de noensinne mistet," gjenløst vår sjel, gjenløst vår kropp, gjenløst alt vi mistet ved fallet. Vår slektning-Gjenløser kom og ble menneske, bodde blant oss, og løftet opp tegnet, og sa: "Det er fullbrakt." Hva er fullbrakt? Alt er fullbrakt. Vi går rett inn i vår arv. Og etter hvert som dagene går, kommer vi nærmere og nærmere.

text:
Boaz and Naomi… After being married for some time, they brought forth a son, which added more to the lineage, and that was Obed. And he also brought forth his son, which was Jesse. And Jesse had eight boys.
And it was the great prophet Samuel who came with the cruse of the oil, the great prophet who went to Jesse, and said, "God has chosen one of your boys to rule and serve My people." And it was out in the backside of the field, in a little shepherd yard, where the little ruddy, scrawny-looking boy was brought: David, the youngest. And Samuel poured the anointing oil on him, in the presence of all of his brothers and them which stood about, and proved that God had anointed him king. Where was that at? Bethlehem. Glory to God in the highest! No wonder, Bethlehem, where he was anointed king.
text_no:
Boas og Naomi… Etter å ha vært gift i en stund, fikk de en sønn som tilføyde mer til slektslinjen, og det var Obed. Deretter fikk Obed en sønn ved navn Isai, og Isai hadde åtte sønner.
Det var den store profeten Samuel som kom med krukken med olje. Han gikk til Isai og sa: "Gud har utvalgt en av dine sønner til å lede og tjene Mitt folk." Det var ute på en åker, i en liten gjetergård, hvor den rødmussete, spe gutten, David, den yngste, ble hentet. Samuel helte salvelsesolje over ham i nærvær av alle hans brødre og de som sto omkring, og beviste at Gud hadde salvet ham til konge. Hvor skjedde dette? Betlehem. Ære være Gud i det høyeste! Ikke rart Betlehem ble valgt, stedet hvor han ble salvet til konge.

text: And it was in Bethlehem, where David was born. And his greater Son, Jesus, was also had to be born in Bethlehem, because there is such a close knit between the two, his Father and Son. Not only was this great Son only the Son, He was the root and the offspring of David, He was even before David, He will be after David, He was for everlasting, from everlasting on. But according to the flesh, and all the things to be fulfilled, He was the Son of David. He was to be born years later in this same Bethlehem, this little forsaken city.
text_no: Det var i Betlehem at David ble født. Og Davids større Sønn, Jesus, måtte også bli født i Betlehem, på grunn av det nære båndet mellom de to, Far og Sønn. Denne store Sønn var ikke bare Sønnen; Han var roten og avkommet til David. Han eksisterte før David, Han vil være etter David, Han er fra evighet til evighet. Men i henhold til kjødet og for å oppfylle alle profetier, var Han Davids Sønn. Mange år senere skulle Han bli født i denne samme lille, forsømte byen Betlehem.

text: But all the time, in there, do you notice, there is working a great mighty mystery that no one seemed to understand. That's the same thing it is in God's Bethlehem today: There is working a mystery sign, but no one seems to understand it. It's something that goes over the top of the heads of the people. They don't seem to get it. No matter what's done, or what's said, and the mysterious part of the things that's done, the people will say, "Oh, well, I guess it's all right," and go on, but they don't understand it. They can't catch it. They can't grasp it. And that's what God was doing in Bethlehem in Judea. He's working, all these little things moving up, to come to one great head.
text_no: Men hele tiden, der inne, legger du merke til at det er et stort og mektig mysterium som ingen ser ut til å forstå. Det samme gjelder i Guds Betlehem i dag: Det arbeider et mysterietegn, men ingen synes å forstå det. Det er noe som går over hodet på folk. De ser ikke ut til å få tak i det. Uansett hva som gjøres eller sies, og uansett hvor mysteriefullt det som gjøres er, sier folk: "Å, vel, jeg antar det er greit," og går videre, men de forstår det ikke. De kan ikke fange det. De kan ikke gripe det. Og det var det Gud gjorde i Betlehem i Judea. Han arbeider, alle disse små tingene samler seg, for å komme til ett stort høydepunkt.

text: David, oh, when he was anointed king, as a little boy, he was ruddy to look up at, but he must have been something in him that looked real to God. The little bitty, the smallest of the family. The rest of the boys, great big fine men, would look good in the robes, and a crown on their head. But God showed what He looks at: not the outward appearance, but the inside of a man. He looked at his heart; He knew what was in David's heart, no matter how the crown looked on him. He knowed He was finding Him a man that was a man after His own heart, which David would be a man after His own heart. That's why He poured the anointing oil … or, had it poured upon David. Which, the name David means "beloved."
text_no: David, da han ble salvet til konge som en liten gutt, var rødmusset i ansiktet, men det var noe med ham som virket ekte for Gud. Han var den minste i familien. De andre guttene var store, flotte menn som ville sett bra ut i kongelige klær og med en krone på hodet. Men Gud viste hva Han ser etter: ikke det ytre utseendet, men det indre i en mann. Han så på hjertet hans; Han visste hva som var i Davids hjerte, uavhengig av hvordan kronen ville se ut på ham. Han visste at Han fant En mann som var etter Hans eget hjerte, og David var en mann etter Hans eget hjerte. Derfor ble den salvede oljen øst over David. Navnet David betyr "elskede."

text: And it was well represented in Jesus, the Beloved One, years later; the Son of David, who was to come to fulfill all things that had been promised. This little city of Bethlehem was where this taken place. And it was on them same little Judean hills where David had herded his sheep, many years later, that the angels sang their first noel on the hills of Judea, overlooking Bethlehem. The first noel, "Unto Thee is born in the city of David, Christ the Lord." When angels first made their appearance to sing, it wasn't at Jerusalem, the big church; neither was it at Gilgal; or neither was it at Shiloh, where they'd had the religious denominational worship all the time. But it was in little Bethlehem, where the Spirit of God had been moving in a mysterious way, bringing forth something. It was there.
text_no: Dette ble godt representert i Jesus, den Elskede, mange år senere; Davids Sønn, som skulle komme for å oppfylle alle løfter. Denne lille byen Betlehem var stedet hvor dette skjedde. Og det var på de samme små judeiske høydene hvor David hadde gjettet sine sauer, mange år senere, at englene sang sin første noel med utsikt over Betlehem. Den første noel, "Til Deg er født i Davids by, Kristus Herren." Da englene først viste seg for å synge, var det ikke i Jerusalem, den store menigheten; heller ikke i Gilgal; og heller ikke i Silo, hvor de alltid hadde hatt religiøs konfesjonell tilbedelse. Men det var i lille Betlehem, hvor Guds Ånd hadde beveget Seg på en mystisk måte, og brakte noe frem. Det skjedde der.

text:
It was there where Christ had to come. It was there. It was in that same little city, with a King born, that a mother, virgin, brought forth her first born son. It sheltered, and housed in its little fortress, the King of kings and the Lord of lords. Where not only did Samuel come to pour out the anointing oil, but God poured out upon Him, and upon the world, Christ the Lord. The angels heralded His coming and sang to the shepherds on the hillside, who followed David the king, years before. See the mystery of God, how great it is?
He was born in this great wheat belt, which one wheat comes forth the basic principles of life. And He was the bread of life. "I am the bread of life. He that eats My flesh and drinks My blood has eternal life, and shall never die or come into condemnation, but has passed from death unto life."
text_no:
Her måtte Kristus komme. Det var der. Det var i den samme lille byen, med en kongefødsel, at en jomfru brakte fram sin førstefødte sønn. Den beskyttet og huset Kongenes Konge og Herrenes Herre. Ikke bare kom Samuel dit for å helle ut salvingsoljen, men Gud utøste Ham over verden, Kristus Herren. Englene forkynte Hans komme og sang for gjeterne på åsene, som fulgte David kongen år tidligere. Ser du Guds mysterium, hvor stort det er?
Han ble født i dette store hveteområdet, hvorfra hvete gir de grunnleggende prinsippene for liv. Og Han var livets brød. "Jeg er livets brød. Den som spiser Mitt kjøtt og drikker Mitt blod har evig liv, og skal aldri dø eller komme til fordømmelse, men har gått over fra døden til livet."

text: Bethlehem! The name beth-el. B-e-t-h, beth; beth in the Hebrew word means "house." E-l stands for Elohim, its abbreviation, Elohim means "God." B-e-t-h, beth; E-l, E-l is Elohim, which is God, the House of God, where the bread of life is laying. E-l, h-e-m, finish it out and bring back your l again with your e, means a "loaf of bread," in the Hebrew, El-hem. E-l is God, Elohim. B-e-t-h is… b-e-t-h is "house." E-l is Elohim abbreviation. Then E-l, e-m is "bread." What was He? The house of the bread of God. "The House of God's bread." House, beth; Elohim, God; El, e-m, bread. "The house of God's bread," means Bethlehem.
text_no: Betlehem! Navnet Bethel, B-e-t-h, bet; bet på hebraisk betyr "hus." E-l står for Elohim, en forkortelse for Elohim, som betyr "Gud." B-e-t-h, bet; E-l, E-l er Elohim, som er Gud, altså Guds hus, der livets brød ligger. E-l, h-e-m, fullfør det og ta tilbake l-en med e-en, betyr "brød," på hebraisk, El-hem. E-l er Gud, Elohim. B-e-t-h er "hus." E-l er forkortelse for Elohim. Så E-l, e-m betyr "brød." Hva var Han? Huset med Guds brød. "Guds brødshus." Hus, bet; Elohim, Gud; El, e-m, brød. "Guds brødshus," betyr Betlehem.

text:
Where could He have been born anywhere else but that? But it was hid to everybody but that prophet; he said, "Out of Bethlehem shall come Him." They was looking in Jerusalem; they was looking in all the big Shilohs; they was looking everywhere. But He come from Bethlehem, because it was the house of God's bread of life. He is God's breadbasket to the world. There He was, born in Bethlehem. He could be born nowhere else.
There could be many great things, as I was studying this week of the different spiritual aspects to that, why He must be born in Bethlehem. When I hit these few places, the Holy Spirit just caught me away, and I said, "O God, that's sufficient. I see it now."
He could not be born nowhere else but Bethlehem. It was the place of the bread of the nation. It was the bread of all the house of Israel, come from there. And He was the bread of life that come down from heaven, the spiritual Manna; has to come from Bethlehem, the bread line, the place where bread lays. Bethlehem, the baking of the bread. Now, Jesus being the bread of life, "The man may eat thereof," He said, "and never die."
text_no:
Hvor ellers kunne Han ha blitt født? Men dette var skjult for alle unntatt profeten, som sa: "Fra Betlehem skal Han komme." De lette i Jerusalem, de lette i alle de store Shilohene; de lette overalt. Men Han kom fra Betlehem, fordi det var Guds livsbrøds hus. Han er Guds brødkurv til verden. Der ble Han født, i Betlehem. Han kunne ikke bli født noe annet sted.
Det kan være mange store ting, som jeg studerte denne uken, med forskjellige åndelige aspekter ved hvorfor Han måtte bli født i Betlehem. Da jeg kom til disse få stedene, grep Den Hellige Ånd meg, og jeg sa: "Å Gud, det er tilstrekkelig. Jeg forstår nå."
Han kunne ikke bli født noe annet sted enn Betlehem. Det var nasjonens brøds sted. Hele Israels hus sitt brød kom derfra. Og Han var Livets Brød som kom ned fra himmelen, den åndelige mannaen; måtte komme fra Betlehem, brødlinjen, stedet hvor brødet ligger. Betlehem, der brødet bakes. Nå, Jesus som Livets Brød, "Den som spiser av dette," sa Han, "skal aldri dø."

text:
Another great event that I wouldn't forget to call, that was when David was in his worst of time, when he was a fugitive. He had already been anointed; he knowed what he was to be. He was to be king, God said so, and yet he was hated. He was standing in between two great deep fires; here was the Philistines on one side, after him, here was Saul on the other side. And he was a man without a nation.
Just as the church stands today, the true living church of God, without a denomination or anything else. She stands alone, but yet she's had the anointing poured on her. She knows what she is.
text_no:
Et annet viktig øyeblikk jeg aldri vil glemme å nevne, var da David var i sin verste tid som flyktning. Han var allerede salvet; han visste hva han skulle bli. Han skulle bli konge, Gud hadde sagt det, men likevel var han hatet. Han sto mellom to store trusler; filisterne på den ene siden og Saul på den andre. Han var en mann uten nasjon.
Akkurat som Menigheten står i dag, den sanne levende Menighet av Gud, uten en konfesjon eller noe annet. Hun står alene, men har like fullt fått salvelsen. Hun vet hva hun er.

text:
How in the world can it ever come to pass, the devil on both sides, driving at David? He had taken refuge in strongholds in the wilderness and in caves, trying to hide out, with a little band of faithful warriors, just a few believing like him. But those men believed God, that that would be the king.
So is the believers today, who is hid out from place to place, but yet they know who is coming to be King. I don't care who is going to be president, we know He is coming. And it looks farther away than it ever did, when science is trying to overrule, and say, "They can build a man, they can do this, and they can take a rabbit and take the pollen and make another rabbit, and so forth," trying to disprove God's Word. Yet, there is a people who believe God, who stands just as pat as they ever did. No matter what comes or goes, they still believe God. God is right! They hold to God's unchanging hands. In the midst of battle, in the midst of tears, in the midst of sickness and death, and everything, they still hold to God's unchanging hand. They know that He is coming King.
text_no:
Hvordan i all verden kan det skje at djevelen angriper David fra begge sider? Han hadde søkt tilflukt i sterke fjellborger i ørkenen og i huler, forsøkte å skjule seg med en liten gruppe trofaste krigere, bare noen få som trodde som ham. Men disse mennene trodde på Gud og at han ville bli konge.
Slik er det med troende i dag, som skjuler seg fra sted til sted, men likevel vet at Han kommer som Konge. Jeg bryr meg ikke om hvem som blir president, vi vet at Han kommer. Og selv om det ser fjernere ut enn noen gang, når vitenskapen prøver å overstyre og påstår: "De kan bygge et menneske, de kan gjøre dette, og de kan ta en kanin og ta pollen og lage en annen kanin, og så videre," de prøver å motbevise Guds Ord. Likevel finnes det et folk som tror på Gud, som står like fast som de alltid har gjort. Uansett hva som skjer, så tror de fortsatt på Gud. Gud har rett! De holder fast ved Guds uforanderlige hånd. Midt i kampens hete, i tårer, sykdom og død, i alt, holder de fortsatt fast ved Guds uforanderlige hånd. De vet at Han kommer som Konge.

text: People today laugh and make fun of them, and call them, "holy-rollers." Call them everything they want to, but them warriors of God stands faithful at the post of duty. Might call them a "healing group," you might call them a "bunch of fanatics," or whatever you wish to; they hang to that King. They know He is coming in power. Though they take His name in vain, scoff and make fun, and call the people who believe Him, "outcasts, bunch of backwash," that doesn't bother them a bit; they stay true at the post of duty.
text_no: Folk i dag ler og gjør narr av dem, kaller dem "hellig-rullere" og andre nedsettende betegnelser. Kall dem hva du vil, men disse Guds krigere står trofaste på sin pliktpost. Du kan kalle dem en "helbredelsesgruppe" eller "en gjeng fanatikere," eller hva du ønsker; de holder fast ved Kongen. De vet at Han kommer i kraft. Selv om folk bruker Hans navn forgjeves, håner og gjør narr, og kaller dem som tror på Ham for "utskudd" eller "en gjeng avskum," påvirker det dem ikke; de holder seg trofaste på sin pliktpost.

text:
Those warriors that was with David stayed right by his side. If a Philistine come up, he had to fight. Whoever it was, they were picked on, every side. Poor David, in his mind, all confused; he thought, "How can it be, Lord?"
You know, leaders sometimes go through things that the congregation don't know what they're going through. When you think of promises God has made, then why don't it come to pass? They don't tell their congregation, they don't tell the people they associate with, but there is many flusterations in the heart of a real leader.
text_no:
De krigerne som var med David, forble tett ved hans side. Hvis en filister kom, måtte de kjempe. Hvem det enn var, ble de utfordret fra alle kanter. Stakkars David, forvirret i sitt sinn; han tenkte: "Hvordan kan dette være, Herre?"
Ledere går noen ganger gjennom ting som menigheten ikke vet om. Når du tenker på løftene Gud har gitt, hvorfor skjer de da ikke? De forteller ikke menigheten, de forteller ikke de de omgås, men det er mange frustrasjoner i hjertet til en ekte leder.

text: David sitting there, his throat burning him, it was in the middle of summer. The Philistines was taking advantage of that split between David and Saul. And Saul looking for David everywhere, and the Philistines, also, and the Philistines looking for the Israelites. Talk about a time of confusion; just about like it is now. David taken refuge in this little place, in this little shelter, everywhere he could get to, the little strongholds that he could hold into. Then he got up on the mountain, on that hot, middle of the summer, when the heat was tremendous, his throat parching, and flusterations and fears in his heart, and wondering, "O God, how could it be? You poured that oil upon me, not because I chose myself, but You chose me. Why did You call me from herding the sheep out yonder, and told me You would give me this to serve Your people, and here You've got me between the fires everywhere?" That was going through his heart.
text_no: David satt der, halsen brant, midt på sommeren. Filisterne utnyttet splittelsen mellom David og Saul. Saul lette overalt etter David, og filisterne fulgte etter. Filisterne søkte også etter israelittene. For en tid med forvirring; omtrent som nå. David fant tilflukt på små steder, i små tilfluktsrom, hvor enn han kunne. Han tok også tilflukt på fjellet, midt på sommeren, under den intense heten, med en brennende hals, fylt av uro og frykt. Han undret seg, "O Gud, hvordan kan dette være? Du salvet meg, ikke fordi jeg valgte det, men fordi Du valgte meg. Hvorfor kalte Du meg fra saueflokken i ødemarken og fortalte meg at jeg skulle tjene Ditt folk, og nå er jeg i fare overalt?" Dette strømmet gjennom hjertet hans.

text:
He sat up on the hill and he looked down, and there the Philistines had come in and garrisoned right in Bethlehem, his little home. Then, his little city was under government control of the enemy. Not only that, but his own father's house, Jesse's house, was under bondage to the Philistines. There was his own nation, his own church, against him. Here was the enemy he was fighting, here is the church people he was fighting; not because he wanted to, but because he was forced to do it.
Many times we are forced to do things and say things that we don't want to say (a real spiritual leader), but he's forced to do it. He has to take his side and show his colors. "I'll take the way with the Lord's despised few," said the song writer.
text_no:
Han satt på høyden og så ned, hvor filistrene hadde inntatt og befestet seg i Betlehem, hans lille hjemby. Byen var nå under fiendens kontroll. I tillegg var hans eget hjem, faren Jesses hus, under filistrenes herredømme. Hans egen nasjon og menighet var imot ham. Her var fienden han kjempet mot, og her var menighetsfolkene han kjempet mot. Ikke fordi han ønsket det, men fordi han var tvunget til det.
Ofte blir vi tvunget til å gjøre og si ting vi ikke ønsker (en sann åndelig leder), men han blir tvunget til det. Han må ta sitt standpunkt og vise sine sanne farger. "Jeg vil ta veien med Herrens foraktede få," sa sangforfatteren.

text: So there he was on that hot day, no doubt, walking back and forth, and looking down through that long valley, of about twenty-five miles down there, and back. There was his own father's house in bondage to the Philistines. There was Saul just across yonder, and here come this one, sitting right between, see, to take sides. Seeing the great time that Israel was all broke up, the church broke up in different denominations, as to say.
